policy: A locally archived article or public-domain scan is a research source, not an automatically publishable verse corpus. Publication requires verse-level rights, an exact locator, checked Yorùbá, and practitioner review.

sources.title: Ifa Divination Trays from Isale-Oyo
sources.authors: O. I. Pogoson and A. O. Akande
sources.use: Four source-recorded verses and one closing pattern, non-commercially quoted with page-level attribution. No silent orthographic modernization.

sources.title: Ọ̀rúnmìlà! Nipa
sources.use: Research and controlled transcription only. Exclude missionary framing; verify each Odù assignment, restore orthography with a Yorùbá specialist, create a new translation, and obtain lineage review before publication.

sources.title: Is There Any Evidence for Hell in the Ifá Literary Corpus?
sources.use: Two field-recorded bilingual verses are promising, but obtain or document performer consent and practitioner review before corpus publication.

sources.title: Spirito-Physical Relevance of Iwure in the Growth and Development of Human Beings
sources.use: The Ọ̀sá-Ìrẹtẹ̀ example is a useful comparison with public-domain Lijadu text. Verify lineage, performer rights, transcription, and translation before publication.
